query:
Research the company "molsoncoors".

Research the prospect's company for: Recent earnings misses or P&L gaps, production/manufacturing growth rates in the past 12-18 months, active cost-reduction initiatives or restructuring, recent CEO changes or strategic reviews underway, guidance revisions or withdrawals, multi-function organizational structure (especially in media/marketing, production, pricing, and distribution), ongoing platform launches or product expansion, supply chain or production capacity constraints, margin compression trends, and cross-functional business challenges (especially any public commentary on why functions are hitting KPIs but business outcomes are missing).

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
# Molson Coors Research Report
**Date:** June 12, 2026

## Executive Summary
Molson Coors is undergoing a massive strategic and operational realignment under new CEO Rahul Goyal, who has designated 2026 as a "reset year" amid severe margin compression and persistent volume declines. Over the last six months, the company has issued a significant profit warning for 2026, launched a $450 million cost-reduction program, and aggressively restructured its Americas and European operations. Despite functional successes in pricing and cost discipline, the company is struggling to translate these into broader business outcomes due to surging aluminum costs, supply chain constraints, and structural inflexibility.

## Detailed Analysis

### Executive Leadership & Strategic Overhaul
Molson Coors has initiated sweeping leadership and strategic changes over the past year to combat shrinking margins and volume losses:
*   **Leadership Transition:** Rahul Goyal succeeded Gavin Hattersley as President and CEO on October 1, 2025 [Is Molson Coors’ Leadership Change and Diversification Strategy Shaping the Investment Case for TAP? - Simply Wall St News](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/food-beverage-tobacco/nyse-tap/molson-coors-beverage/news/is-molson-coors-leadership-change-and-diversification-strate). 
*   **Horizon 2030 Strategy:** In early 2026, the company launched the "Horizon 2030" strategic framework, aiming to diversify into a "Beyond Beer" powerhouse and increase "local agility" in decision-making [Molson Coors Unveils 'Horizon 2030' Strategy as Q4 Sales Reach $2.66 Billion](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/marketminute-2026-3-12-molson-coors-unveils-horizon-2030-strategy-as-q4-sales-reach-266-billion). As part of this product expansion, Molson Coors is integrating the recently acquired Atomic Brands (Monaco Cocktails), which is expected to contribute approximately 1% to global net sales revenue [Molson Coors Q1 2026 Earnings Deck](https://s27.q4cdn.com/826627388/files/doc_financials/2026/q1/Molson-Coors-Q1-Earnings-Deck-FINAL-4-30-26.pdf).
*   **Shareholder Actions:** On February 9, 2026, the board doubled its Class B stock repurchase authorization from $2 billion to $4 billion, extending the program through December 2031 [Molson Coors Doubles Buyback to $4B But Forecasts 15% Profit Drop as Beer Industry Reckoning Deepens](https://fintool.com/news/molson-coors-profit-guidance-cut-buyback-doubled). Additionally, Director Andrew Thomas Molson purchased 7,500 shares on the open market in early 2026, increasing his direct ownership by nearly 50% [How Executive Share Buying and Restructuring Moves Could Shape Molson Coors (TAP) Investment Outlook](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/executive-share-buying-restructuring-moves-043356393.html).

### Financial Performance, P&L Gaps & Guidance Revisions
The company has suffered multiple earnings misses and severe margin compression, leading to significant guidance downgrades:
*   **Earnings Misses:** In Q3 2025, Molson Coors missed forecasts with EPS of $1.67 (vs. $1.71 expected) and revenue of $2.97 billion (vs. $3.04 billion expected) [Earnings call transcript: Molson Coors Q3 2025 misses forecasts, stock steady By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-molson-coors-q3-2025-misses-forecasts-stock-steady-93CH-4331150). Q4 2025 revenue also missed estimates, declining 2.7% to $2.66 billion against a $2.71 billion consensus [Molson Coors Q4 Earnings Beat Estimates, 2026 View Soft](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/molson-coors-q4-earnings-beat-170200066.html). 
*   **Massive Impairment:** The company reported a Q3 2025 net loss of $2.93 billion [How Impairment-Driven Losses and Lowered Sales Guidance at Molson Coors (TAP) Has Changed Its Investment Story](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/impairment-driven-losses-lowered-sales-030914666.html). This was driven by a $3.645 billion goodwill and intangible asset impairment loss for the Americas reporting unit, triggered when the unit's carrying value exceeded its fair value [FORM 10-K Molson Coors Beverage Company](https://s27.q4cdn.com/826627388/files/doc_financials/2025/q4/827fc683-4e6d-4e07-8b98-85ac4b263b7d.pdf).
*   **Margin Compression:** Molson Coors is battling a negative net margin of 15.89% (cited as high as 16.41% following Q1 2026) [Morgan Stanley Issues Positive Forecast for Molson Coors Beverage (NYSE:TAP) Stock Price - Daily Political](https://www.dailypolitical.com/2026/05/01/morgan-stanley-issues-positive-forecast-for-molson-coors-beverage-nysetap-stock-price.html). A primary driver is an $80 million headwind from Midwest Premium aluminum cost inflation—exacerbated by 50% US import tariffs—which caused an 8.1% jump in cost of goods sold (COGS) per hectoliter [Molson Coors Q4 2025 slides: profit warning for 2026 amid cost surge By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/company-news/molson-coors-q4-2025-slides-profit-warning-for-2026-amid-cost-surge-93CH-4516824); [Molson Coors cuts US workforce in restructuring | Latest Market News](https://www.argusmedia.com/en/news-and-insights/latest-market-news/2744184-molson-coors-cuts-us-workforce-in-restructuring).
*   **Guidance Withdrawals/Revisions:** The company issued a severe profit warning for 2026, projecting adjusted EPS to fall 11% to 15% (guidance of 4.607-4.823), drastically below the consensus analyst estimate of 5.420 to 5.48 [Molson Coors forecasts sharp drop in 2026 profit as aluminum costs bite | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/retail-consumer/molson-coors-forecasts-sharp-drop-2026-profit-aluminum-costs-bite-2026-02-18/); [Molson Coors Beverage (NYSE:TAP) Issues FY 2026 Earnings Guidance - Ticker Report](https://www.tickerreport.com/banking-finance/13351406/molson-coors-beverage-nysetap-issues-fy-2026-earnings-guidance.html). 

### Production, Supply Chain & Capacity Constraints
Molson Coors has faced two consecutive years of falling unit sales, negative return on invested capital (ROIC), and ongoing supply chain disruptions [Should Weak Sales, Margins, and Returns Require Action From Molson Coors Beverage (TAP) Investors?](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/weak-sales-margins-returns-require-141040339.html).
*   **Volume Declines:** FY 2025 net sales fell 4.2% to $11.14 billion [Molson Coors to close UK's Sharp's Brewery - Just Drinks](https://www.just-drinks.com/news/molson-coors-to-close-sharps-brewery-in-uk/). In Q4 2025, sales volumes dropped 8.5% year-over-year [Molson Coors (NYSE:TAP) Misses Q4 CY2025 Revenue Estimates, Stock Drops - StockStory](https://stockstory.org/us/stocks/nyse/tap/news/earnings/molson-coors-nysetap-misses-q4-cy2025-revenue-estimates-stock-drops). 
*   **Supply Chain Constraints:** The company issued Q2 2026 guidance anticipating a 6% to 9% decline in U.S. shipments due to inventory dynamics, planned brewery upgrades, and a specific disruption in glass supplies [Molson Coors (TAP) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript | The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/04/30/molson-coors-tap-q1-2026-earnings-transcript/); [Coors Faces Supply Chain Challenges as Glass Shortage Hits Beer Industry - Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-30/molson-coors-beer-shipments-hampered-by-glass-supply-shortage).
*   **Manufacturing Upgrades:** To improve efficiency, Molson Coors completed a 200,000-square-foot brewery expansion in Golden, Colorado in early 2026, expected to lower energy use by up to 30% [Molson Coors completes 4-year upgrade of Colorado brewery | Food Dive](https://www.fooddive.com/news/molson-coors-golden-colorado-brewery-expansion-completed-manufacturing-beer-carbon-footprint/730287/). They also partnered with o9 Solutions to transform enterprise planning capabilities (demand, supply, and financial) in the Americas [o9 Partners With Molson Coors on a Multi-Year Initiative to Transform its Enterprise Planning Capabilities in the Americas](https://o9solutions.com/news/o9-partners-with-molson-coors-on-a-multi-year-initiative-to-transform-its-enterprise-planning-capabilities-in-the-americas).

### Restructuring & Cost-Reduction Initiatives
To mitigate margin pressure, Molson Coors announced a comprehensive cost savings program on February 18, 2026, targeting up to $450 million in cumulative savings through 2028 [Molson Coors Beverage Company Reports 2025 Fourth Quarter and Full Year Results](https://ir.molsoncoors.com/news/news-details/2026/Molson-Coors-Beverage-Company-Reports-2025-Fourth-Quarter-and-Full-Year-Results/default.aspx).
*   **Americas Restructuring:** In late 2025, the company eliminated approximately 400 salaried positions (9% of its regional salaried workforce), incurring $35 million to $50 million in one-time restructuring charges [Molson Coors Reorganizes to Accelerate Growth](https://www.mheducation.com/content/mheducation/us/en/highered/blog/2026/01/molson-coors-reorganizes-to-accelerate-growth.html); [Molson Coors Beverage Company Announces Corporate Restructuring of Americas Business Unit](https://ir.molsoncoors.com/news/news-details/2025/Molson-Coors-Beverage-Company-Announces-Corporate-Restructuring-of-Americas-Business-Unit/default.aspx).
*   **Brewery Closures:** The company permanently closed the Jacob Leinenkugel Brewing Company (Chippewa Falls, WI) and the 10th Street Brewery (Milwaukee) on January 17, 2025 [Molson Coors Shutting Down 157-Year-Old Wisconsin Brewery](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/molson-coors-shutting-down-157-165905520.html). It has also proposed closing Sharp’s Brewery (Cornwall, UK) and a national contact centre in Cardiff by the end of 2026, risking 200 jobs [Molson Coors Shuts Sharp's Brewery Weighing Cost Savings Against Brand Value - Simply Wall St News](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/food-beverage-tobacco/nyse-tap/molson-coors-beverage/news/molson-coors-shuts-sharps-brewery-weighing-cost-savings-agai).

### Organizational Structure & Cross-Functional Challenges
Molson Coors is actively battling structural bloat and a disconnect between functional KPIs and overall business outcomes.
*   **KPI vs. Outcome Disconnect:** Management has highlighted "conflicting currents" within the business. For example, in Q1 2026, strong pricing power and cost discipline allowed EPS to surge 24%, hitting functional targets. However, this failed to energize investors or drive business growth because overall volumes still declined 3.1% across major markets, and macro cost pressures erased the gains [Molson Coors Q1 2026 slides: EPS surges 24% amid volume declines By Investing.com](https://in.investing.com/news/company-news/molson-coors-q1-2026-slides-eps-surges-24-amid-volume-declines-93CH-5393335). CFO Tracey Joubert noted that capability investments are now strictly viewed through a "lens of productivity, efficiency or to enable top and bottom line" to ensure functional KPIs align with actual business outcomes [Earnings call transcript: Molson Coors Q3 2025 misses forecasts, stock steady By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-molson-coors-q3-2025-misses-forecasts-stock-steady-93CH-4331150).
*   **Structural Bloat:** Internal efficiency initiatives have ironically created organizational drag. The IT PMO and New Product Packages (NPP) organizations were considered internal "success stories," but their combined success led to the creation of additional layers (a U.S.-divisional PMO and a Global PMO), introducing "unwanted inflexibility" that made components difficult to sell profitably [The Molson Coors Operational Portfolio Architecture: A Case Study](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/228350079_The_Molson_Coors_Operational_Portfolio_Architecture_A_Case_Study).
*   **Reorganization for Speed:** The company eliminated the Chief Commercial Officer role in late 2025 [Molson Coors eyes M&A to build out beyond beer portfolio | Food Dive](https://www.fooddive.com/news/molson-coors-mergers-acquisitions-strategy/804885/). It is currently "unwinding" its Americas Commercial Organization to reduce layers and move profit-and-loss accountability closer to regional markets [Michelle St. Jacques to Exit Molson Coors; Company Realigns Leadership Team & Organizational Structure | Brewbound](https://www.brewbound.com/news/michelle-st-jacques-to-exit-molson-coors-company-realigns-leadership-team-organizational-structure/). The goal is to bridge the gap between marketing insights and supply chain execution, utilizing "precision marketing" and "test and learn" approaches to reduce innovation-to-market time from 18 months to 4 months [Molson Coors Announces Revitalization Plan and Reports 2019 Third Quarter Results | Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20191030005348/en/Molson-Coors-Announces-Revitalization-Plan-and-Reports-2019-Third-Quarter-Results).

## Gaps and Limitations
Based on the provided findings, the following requested information could not be fully addressed:
*   **Specific Media/Marketing KPIs:** While there is commentary on marketing speed (reducing innovation time to 4 months), specific metrics regarding media spend efficiency, ROAS, or active marketing campaign KPIs were not available.
*   **Detailed Pricing Strategies:** The findings note "strong pricing power" as a functional success, but lack granular data on specific pricing tier adjustments or promotional strategies.
*   **Broader Product Expansion Details:** Aside from the integration of Atomic Brands (Monaco Cocktails) and the general "Beyond Beer" strategy, specific names or timelines of other ongoing platform launches were not provided.
